p.strong: Verification that tail/head haven't changed between reading pointer and reading its next field

div.p: Snapshot #1 Capture current value of tail (or head for dequeue)
Dereference Access tail->next ⚠️ RACE WINDOW Another thread may modify queue->tail here!
Snapshot #2 Read tail again to compare

Thread A Thread B ───────── ───────── tail = load(&queue->tail) → ptr_A CAS(&queue->tail, ptr_A, ptr_B) → SUCCESS (tail now points to B) next = load(&tail->next) → ptr_A->next (tail was ptr_A when we read it!) current = load(&queue->tail) → ptr_B tail != current → INVALID, must retry
Without this check, we might dereference ptr_A->next after ptr_A has been removed from the queue, leading to use-after-free.
